# Riz

> Self-hosted AWS Lambda + API Gateway v2 runtime. Run your Lambda functions on your own box, unmodified. Single Rust binary. Drop-in compatibility with the AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP event payload ‚Äî handlers written for AWS Lambda compile and run on Riz with zero changes.

Riz is an open-source self-hosted runtime that emulates AWS Lambda + API Gateway v2. You point your `riz.toml` at your handler files; Riz spawns one isolated child-process pool per function, dispatches HTTP requests using the canonical AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP payload shape (via the `aws_lambda_events` crate), and exposes a ratatui terminal dashboard with real percentile latency tracking (P50/P75/P90/P95/P99 over a 5-minute rolling window), per-route + per-function Prometheus metrics, hot-reload of config, zero-downtime hot-swap deploys from S3, and a fully MCP-spec-compliant JSON-RPC server that turns every function into an LLM-callable tool.

Function-centric config matches AWS exactly: one `[function.<name>]` block per function, one process pool, N declared routes per function. AWS-style path syntax (`{id}`, `{proxy+}`), AWS-style handler resolution (`handler = "index.handler"` splits into file + export name), AWS-style `$default` catch-all route key, AWS-style `stage` and `stage_variables`, AWS-style `integration_timeout_ms` separate from handler `timeout_ms`. Handlers receive the event in the exact shape AWS sends ‚Äî same crate, same field names, same serde JSON.

## Author

- [Chris Rizzuto](https://linkedin.com/in/ship247) ‚Äî chris@riz.dev ‚Äî 20 years engineering leadership at Meta, Google, Microsoft, Oracle.

## Project

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/crizzuto/riz): source, issues, releases
- [Releases](https://github.com/crizzuto/riz/releases): prebuilt binaries for Linux and macOS (x86_64 + aarch64)
- [Install script](https://riz.dev/install): one-line install ‚Äî `curl -fsSL https://riz.dev/install | sh`

## What works today (v0.1)

- **AWS API Gateway v2 HTTP payload** ‚Äî exact `aws_lambda_events::apigw::ApiGatewayV2httpRequest`/`Response` types, including `cookies`, `queryStringParameters`, `multiValueHeaders`, full `requestContext` with `time`, `timeEpoch`, `userAgent`, `stage`, `accountId`, etc.
- **Function-centric config** ‚Äî `[function.<name>]` blocks with optional `[[function.<name>.routes]]`, mirroring AWS Lambda's "one function = one execution environment" model
- **Bun runtime** (TypeScript / JavaScript)
- **Python runtime** ‚Äî `runtime = "python"`, `handler = "app.lambda_handler"` resolves to file `app.py` + attribute `lambda_handler`. Subprocess adapter embedded in the binary.
- **Rust runtime** ‚Äî `runtime = "rust"`, `handler = "./target/release/my-handler"` invokes the pre-compiled binary speaking line-delimited JSON stdin/stdout. Reference crate `riz-rust-runtime` provides the boilerplate.
- **AWS-style handler syntax** ‚Äî `handler = "index.handler"` resolves to file `index.ts` + export `handler`; explicit path form also supported
- **AWS path patterns** ‚Äî `{name}` single-segment captures, `{proxy+}` greedy multi-segment captures, `$default` HTTP catch-all
- **`ANY` method** ‚Äî matches all HTTP verbs (AWS API GW v2 semantic)
- **Separate `timeout_ms` and `integration_timeout_ms`** ‚Äî handler-side vs gateway-side limits, matching AWS Lambda + API Gateway behavior
- **`stage_variables`** per function, surfaced as `event.stageVariables`
- **Auth-aware response cache** ‚Äî automatically bypassed when `Authorization` or `Cookie` headers are present
- **Hot-reload** of `riz.toml` with diff-aware function add/remove/replace
- **Hot-swap deploys** from S3 that drain in-flight invocations before killing old processes
- **Per-function process isolation** with semaphore-bounded concurrency, automatic respawn on crash/timeout, liveness watcher
- **Terminal dashboard** (ratatui) ‚Äî real nearest-rank P50/P75/P90/P95/P99 over a 5-minute rolling window, plus host process stats and system endpoint visibility
- **Prometheus metrics** at `/_riz/metrics` ‚Äî `riz_invocations_total`, `riz_errors_total`, `riz_latency_ms` (summary), `riz_cold_starts_total`, `riz_function_healthy`, `riz_uptime_seconds`
- **MCP server** at `/_riz/mcp` ‚Äî spec-compliant JSON-RPC 2.0 (`initialize`, `notifications/initialized`, `ping`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`, `resources/list`, `prompts/list`, batch). Every user function becomes an LLM-invokable tool with no code changes.
- **Health endpoints** ‚Äî `/health` (liveness 200), `/ready` (per-pool readiness), `/_riz/health` (rich per-function status)
- **30-second graceful drain** on SIGTERM/SIGINT (hard cap enforced)
- **Single Rust binary** with embedded Bun and Python adapters (no install dance)
- **Datadog metrics emitter** alongside Prometheus
- **WebSocket APIs** ‚Äî `$connect` / `$disconnect` / `$default` lifecycle events shaped as `ApiGatewayWebsocketProxyRequest`. Built-in `@connections` management API at `/_riz/connections/{id}` (GET / POST / DELETE) for handlers to push messages back to connected clients.
- **Lambda authorizers** ‚Äî REQUEST type (calls a user-declared function) and JWT type (validates against a JWKS URL). Responses cached by source IP + Authorization header hash for configurable TTL (default 5 min). `requestContext.authorizer` populated on success.
- **CORS auto-preflight** ‚Äî `[cors]` config block (global + per-function override). OPTIONS requests return 204 with `Access-Control-Allow-*` headers; non-OPTIONS requests get origin echoed when in allowlist.
- **Bearer-token auth on `/_riz/*`** ‚Äî set `RIZ_AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN` or `[auth] bearer_token` in `riz.toml`. `/_riz/health` always open (liveness probes). Constant-time token comparison.
- **Real Lambda context** ‚Äî `getRemainingTimeInMillis()` uses the actual per-request deadline, `functionName` from `riz.toml`, `invokedFunctionArn` is a synthetic `arn:riz:lambda:local:...` ARN, `awsRequestId` matches `event.requestContext.requestId`.
- **On-box safety profile (always-on)** ‚Äî every spawned child gets `RLIMIT_CORE=0` (no core-dump disk fill), `RLIMIT_NOFILE=4096` (FD-leak cap), `RLIMIT_FSIZE=100MiB` (single-write cap). Linux only: `PR_SET_PDEATHSIG(SIGKILL)` (kernel SIGKILLs orphan children if riz daemon dies), `PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS` (privilege escalation downgrade), `RLIMIT_NPROC=256` (fork-bomb cap). Applied in `pre_exec` after fork, before execve.
- **Opt-in per-function safety caps** ‚Äî `memory_mb` field on `[function.<name>]` maps to `RLIMIT_AS` (mirrors AWS Lambda's `MemorySize`). `cpu_time_secs` maps to `RLIMIT_CPU` (kills runaway loops via SIGXCPU then SIGKILL). `allowed_paths = ["./handler", "/tmp"]` enforces a Linux Landlock filesystem allowlist (kernel 5.13+).
- **WebSocket `@connections` list endpoint** ‚Äî `GET /_riz/connections` returns a JSON array of `{connectionId, function, connectedAgeSecs}` for all live WS connections. Operator tool for inspecting state.

## Coming (v0.2 and beyond)

- Non-HTTP event sources (SQS, SNS, S3, EventBridge, scheduled) ‚Äî requires polling/push-source adapters; different product surface
- Node.js runtime adapter

## Requirements

- **Runtime dependency**: Bun must be installed on PATH (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`) ‚Äî Riz spawns Bun processes to execute TypeScript/JavaScript handlers
- **Linux or macOS** (x86_64 or aarch64). Windows not supported.
